county.source: Allegheny County Act 50 homestead exclusion (county real estate tax)
county.notes: County applies a $18,000 reduction in assessed value before county millage.

Aleppo Township.source: Assumed municipality default ($18,000) — Act 50 adoption not verified for this jurisdiction
Aleppo Township.notes: Many Allegheny taxing bodies use the $18,000 exclusion when they adopt Act 50. Municipal participation varies; some set other amounts (e.g. Pittsburgh $15,000, Pine Township sets an annual maximum). Confirm with the jurisdiction before relying on this figure.

City of Pittsburgh.source: City of Pittsburgh Act 50 homestead (owner-occupied primary residence)
City of Pittsburgh.notes: Pittsburgh city homestead is lower than the county default. Parks and library taxes use the same homestead base in city law; this app models city millage only.

North Allegheny.source: North Allegheny District Budget & Tax Information
North Allegheny.notes: For 2026–27, the exact exclusion will not be known until North Allegheny adopts its Act 1 resolution after receiving its state gaming allocation and final homestead counts. In the proposed budget, the exclusion was $11,938.29.

North Hills.notes: The North Hills School District Board of Education approved the final budget for the 2026-27 school year at its meeting on June 4, 2026

metadata.disclaimer: Reference table for Act 50 homestead exclusions on assessed value. Only taxing bodies that adopted an exclusion apply it; applications are filed with Allegheny County by March 1. Amounts marked default are placeholders until confirmed from district resolutions. This is separate from Act 1 gaming-funded school tax credits on bills.
metadata.school_homestead_method: School District Allocation divided by homestead count, divided by 2026-2027 millage, multiplied by 1,000
